tone_examples: Bridal season is starting — bookings usually 2x normal in next 4 weeks
Quick one — your Saturday 5-7pm slot has been the strongest this month

digest.title: Olaplex No.9 launches in India — bond protector for chemically-treated hair
digest.summary: Pre-shampoo bond protector targeting damage from frequent colour and smoothening. Distributors confirmed in Delhi/Mumbai/Bangalore. Wholesale ₹3,200/bottle, retail ~₹4,500.
digest.actionable: Worth piloting if your colour client mix is >25%

digest.title: Formaldehyde-free smoothening alternatives gaining share — citric-acid based
digest.summary: Brazilian citric-acid keratin alternatives now widely available. Lower fume, comparable hold for ~6-8 weeks. Pricing roughly +15% over standard keratin.
digest.actionable: Position as 'safe smoothening' for pregnancy / asthma clients

digest.title: Wedding season opener — first lean April-May window before main Oct-Dec rush
digest.summary: Mini-bridal window April-May (~15% of annual bridal volume) often missed by salons focused on Oct-Dec. Pre-wedding skincare + mehendi-prep services peak now.
digest.actionable: Run a 'Bridal Trial @ ₹999' offer; book 2-month skincare package

digest.title: L'Oreal Professionnel India training: Advanced Balayage Masterclass
digest.summary: 2-day in-person training in Mumbai. ₹15,000 fee. Targeted at stylists with 2+ years of colour experience.
digest.actionable: Worth subsidising for one stylist if balayage demand is rising in your locality

digest.title: 'Walk-in available' tag on GBP boosting calls 23% in metros
digest.summary: Salons that explicitly add 'walk-in available' to their GBP description see 23% higher call volume in the same locality vs salons without. Effect strongest weekday afternoons.
digest.actionable: Add the tag to your GBP description this week

patient_content_library.title: Is keratin safe? What to ask before booking
patient_content_library.body: Old-school keratin uses formaldehyde — safe in trained hands but not for pregnancy or asthma. Citric-acid alternatives exist now with similar hold. Two questions to ask any salon: (1) what's your activator? (2) is the room ventilated? If you don't get a clear answer, look elsewhere.

patient_content_library.title: Balayage vs highlights — which actually suits you?
patient_content_library.body: Highlights are uniform stripes; balayage is hand-painted, growing out softly with no visible regrowth line. Highlights cost less but need touch-ups every 8 weeks. Balayage holds 4-5 months. If you don't visit the salon often, balayage is the practical choice.

patient_content_library.title: Post-colour care — first 72 hours matter most
patient_content_library.body: Three rules: no shampoo for 48h (let cuticle close), use sulphate-free shampoo from day 3, and avoid hot water washes for the first month. Skip these and you'll lose 30% of the colour vibrance in the first wash.

seasonal_beats.note: primary wedding/festival season — bridal package bookings 4x baseline
secondary bridal window + summer hair-care surge
Holi colour-recovery surge — book hair spas the week after
